title: Observation of the Optical Phonons in α-MnTe films
authors: Himanshu Sheokand, Arun K Kumay, Mazharul Islam Mondal, Milo Sprague, Ravinder Sharma, Jayan Thomas, Dariusz Kaczorowski, Andrzej Ptok, Madhab Neupane
abstract: The altermagnetic materials have emerged as model systems for studying spin split electronic structures, yet controlled epitaxial growth on technologically relevant substrates remains challenging. Among the known candidates, MnTe stands out as a prominent altermagnetic material owing to its layered structure and high Neel temperature. Here, we report the molecular beam epitaxy (MBE) growth of high quality alpha MnTe thin films on GaAs(111)B substrates and provide a comprehensive analysis of the growth evolution and structural properties. Raman spectroscopy reveals multiple vibrational features of alpha MnTe including modes near 121, and 140 1/cm. Combined with first principles phonon calculations, these features are identified as the Raman-active phonons of the hexagonal NiAs type lattice. Our results show that the high crystalline quality of MBE grown alpha MnTe enables the complete experimental resolution of all symmetry allowed Raman active phonon modes, highlighting epitaxial alpha MnTe as a robust thin film platform for investigating altermagnetism and its lattice coupled excitations.
summary: The researchers successfully grew high-quality alpha-MnTe thin films using molecular beam epitaxy on GaAs substrates. By using Raman spectroscopy and first-principles calculations, they identified the key optical phonon modes, establishing these films as a reliable platform for studying altermagnetism and lattice-coupled excitations.
main_problem: The challenge of achieving controlled epitaxial growth of altermagnetic MnTe thin films on technologically relevant substrates and characterizing their vibrational properties.
main_result: Successful MBE growth of high-quality, stoichiometric alpha-MnTe thin films on GaAs(111)B was achieved, with Raman spectroscopy resolving all symmetry-allowed Raman-active phonon modes.
method: Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE) for growth, combined with RHEED, XRD, SEM, and EDX for structural characterization, and Raman spectroscopy paired with first-principles phonon calculations for vibrational analysis.
model_or_system: Epitaxial alpha-MnTe thin films with a hexagonal NiAs-type structure grown on GaAs(111)B substrates.
key_observables: Raman-active phonon modes (121 and 140 cm-1), Mn:Te stoichiometry, and crystal orientation/structural integrity.
important_parameters: Substrate temperature of 425 degrees C, Te-rich flux ratio of approximately 1:6, and film thickness of approximately 40 nm.
main_assumptions: The monolayer calculation assumes a stable 5-atom unit cell; interface calculations assume a specific 15-atom primitive cell model.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the MnTe crystal structure, XRD patterns, and RHEED evolution; Fig 2 presents SEM images and EDX elemental maps; Fig 3 displays Raman spectra for films and substrates and theoretical phonon dispersion/displacements; Fig 4 shows the interface model and projected phonon dispersion.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the importance of altermagnetic MnTe, details the MBE growth process and structural characterization, presents Raman spectroscopy results and identifies phonon modes via first-principles calculations, and concludes with the implications for altermagnetism research.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a,b) Crystal structure of hexagonal α-MnTe shown along two different crystallographic directions, illustrating the atomic arrangement and stacking sequence of alternating Mn and Te layers consistent with the NiAs-type structure. (c) X- ray diffraction pattern plotted as intensity versus 2θ scan over the range of 10°–80°, showing the GaAs(111) and GaAs(222) substrate reflections together with the MnTe (0002) and (0004) film peaks. The observation of only (000ℓ) MnTe reflections confirms highly oriented growth with the c-axis of α-MnTe aligned normal to the GaAs(111) substrate surface. (d) RHEED patterns recorded during the growth of epitaxial α-MnTe on GaAs(111)B, illustrating the...

Fig. 1 summarizes the structural characterization of the epitaxial α-MnTe thin film grown on GaAs(111). Figs. 1(a) and 1(b) shows the crystal structure of hexag- onal α-MnTe, illustrating the atomic arrangement and stacking sequence viewed along two different crystallo- graphic directions. The structure consists of alternating Mn and Te layers arranged in a hexagonal lattice, consis- tent with the NiAs-type structure. Fig. 1(c) presents the XRD pattern plotted as intensity versus 2θ scan collected over the range 10°–80°, confirming the high crystallinity and phase purity of the film. The diffraction pattern exhibits pronounced substrate reflections corresponding to GaAs(111) and GaAs(222),...

FIG. 2. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) and energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDX) analysis of α-MnTe/GaAs(111)B. (a) SEM image of a selected region of the 10 µm MnTe film where the EDX spectra were acquired. (b) Elemental maps show uniform Mn and Te distribution along with strong Ga and As signals from the substrate. (c) Quantitative EDX yields a near-ideal stoichiometric Mn:Te ratio (∼1:1).

FIG. 3. (a) Raman spectra of α-MnTe film on GaAs(111)B surface. The results displayed three vibrational modes, from which 121 cm−1 and 140 cm−1 belong to the α-MnTe and the 268 cm−1 peak belongs to the GaAs(111). (b) Raman spectra of bare GaAs(111)B surface. (c) Theoretically obtained phonon dispersion for α-MnTe monolayer. Inset show corresponding two dimensional Brillouin zone with high symmetry points. (d) Displacements given be the four Raman active modes for MnTe monolayer. Raman active modes are also marked by blue points on panel (c).

FIG. 4. (a) Investigated α-MnTe/GaAs(111)B inter- face. α-MnTe monolayer is deposited on GaAs(111)B sufrace. (b) Projection of the phonon dispersion of the α- MnTe/GaAs(111)B interface on the Mn-Te atoms contribu- tion.
